rtl_signal_names:
// Spec: "write data bus"
    output reg  [31:0] PRDATA

// Spec: "read data bus"
    output wire        PREADY

// Spec: "master data output"
    input  wire        MISO

// Spec: "master data input"
    output reg         SS_N

// Spec: "interrupt request"
    output wire        WE_N       // Spec: "write enable strobe" (active-low)

alias_table_unique_entries.reasoning: The system clock signal is the primary clock input used for all synchronous logic operations, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.pclk.
The system reset signal is asynchronous and active-low, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.nrst.
The peripheral select signal is used to select the SDC as an APB slave, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.psel.
The transfer enable signal marks the second cycle of an APB transfer, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.penable.
The write enable signal is used to indicate write operation, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.pwrite.
The register address signal selects the target register for APB transfers, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.paddr.
The write data bus signal carries 32-bit data from the master to SDC during writes, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.pwdata.
The read data bus signal carries 32-bit data from SDC to the master during reads, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.prdata.
The transfer acknowledge signal is asserted high by SDC to complete APB transactions, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.pready.
The serial clock signal is generated by SDC, with frequency set by the baud rate divisor, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.sck.
The master data output signal is serial data to the external slave, MSB first, shifted on the falling clock edge, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.mosi.
The master data input signal is serial data from the external slave, sampled on the rising clock edge, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.miso.
The chip select signal is an active-low output driven low at the start of a frame to address the external slave, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.ss_n.
The interrupt request signal is asserted when transmission complete or parity error is detected, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.irq_out.
The write enable signal is used to indicate write operation, generating an active-low write strobe when asserted, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.we_n.
The receive clock signal is used for reception operations, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.clk.
The transmit clock signal is used for transmission operations, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.clk.
The receive reset signal is used to reset the receiver, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.rst_n.
The transmit reset signal is used to reset the transmitter, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.rst_n.
The transmit start signal may be related to the transmission complete signal, but the naming convention is different.
The flow control enable signal activates CTS/RTS-style handshaking when set in the control register, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.fc_en.
The baud rate divisor signal sets the system clock divisor for SPI clock generation, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.baud_div.
The transmit data signal is serial data to the external slave, MSB first, shifted on the falling clock edge, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.tx_data.
The transmit serial clock signal is generated by SDC, with frequency set by the baud rate divisor, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.sck_out.
The transmit master data output signal is serial data to the external slave, MSB first, shifted on the falling clock edge, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.mosi_out.
The transmit done signal is pulsed for one clock cycle when the last bit of a frame has been shifted out, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.tx_done.
The FIFO empty signal is asserted when the transmit buffer has no pending data and is ready for a new frame, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.fifo_empty.
The transmit acknowledge signal is set when the remote slave confirms receipt of the last transmitted byte, matching the RTL signal sdc_tx.ack_flag.
The receive master data input signal is serial data from the external slave, sampled on the rising clock edge, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.miso_in.
The receive serial clock signal is generated by the external slave, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.sck_in.
The data valid signal is asserted for one cycle when a new received byte is available in the receive buffer, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.dv.
The parity error flag signal is set and held when a parity mismatch is detected on the received byte, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.par_err.
The receive buffer full signal is an active-low flag, deasserted when the receive buffer cannot accept additional data, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.buf_full_n.
The receive data signal is serial data from the external slave, sampled on the rising clock edge, matching the RTL signal sdc_rx.rx_data.

The write data bus signal carries 32-bit data from the master to SDC during writes, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.pwdata.

per_signal_decisions.rtl_signal_raw:
// Spec: "write data bus"
    output reg  [31:0] PRDATA

alignment.reasoning: The read data bus signal carries 32-bit data from SDC to the master during reads, matching the RTL signal sdc_top.prdata.

per_signal_decisions.rtl_signal_raw:
// Spec: "read data bus"
    output wire        PREADY
